item.title: FRCS clarifies an immediate Departure Order was issued after revocation of previous order for man linked to alleged $20M tax fraud
Police continue search for 18 missing people
item.description: Police say the search continues for 18 people who are currently reported missing across the country. They say officers are continuing to gather information that could help locate the missing people, but so far none have been found.
item.content:encoded:
Police say the search continues for 18 people who are currently reported missing across the country.

They say officers are continuing to gather information that could help locate the missing people, but so far none have been found.

Those still missing are 78-year-old Singa Mani, who was reported missing at Nakasi Police Station and has been missing from home since 3rd February; 17-year-old Shimran Kumar, reported missing at Nakasi Police Station since 15th February; 19-year-old Nacanieli Ratumara, reported missing at Nausori Police Station since 13th March; 49-year-old Zareena Bibi, reported missing at Totogo Police Station since 19th February; 39-year-old Kriteshni Sanjana Goundar, reported missing at Labasa Police Station since 27th March; 15-year-old Maria Vuniadi, reported missing at Labasa Police Station since 11th April; 17-year-old Torika Dikainona, reported missing at Labasa Police Station since 11th April; 16-year-old Mere Divoce Tasere, reported missing at Korovou Police Station since 16th April; 29-year-old Vetaia Saunibula, reported missing at Lautoka Police Station since 20th April; 17-year-old Salote Adibuli Tukana, reported missing at Totogo Police Station since 30th April; 16-year-old Salaseini Nacika, reported missing at Nakasi Police Station since 29th April; 84-year-old Kitione Botitu, reported missing at Ba Police Station since 24th April; 45-year-old Usenia Ceinalewa, reported missing at Sigatoka Police Station since 9th April; 28-year-old Timaio Lanyon, reported missing at Tukavesi Police Station since 19th May; 13-year-old Meredani Moce, reported missing at Nadi Police Station since 21st May; 18-year-old Sereima Lalabalavu and 16-year-old Elenoa Veresa, both reported missing at Korovou Police Station since 21st March; and 16-year-old Jone Lutudromu, reported missing at Lautoka Police Station since 19th June.

Police are urging anyone with information about the whereabouts of any of the missing people to contact Crime Stoppers on 919 or 917.

item.title: Husband appeals for help after wife allegedly taken by force in Nakasi
item.description: The husband of a woman who has been missing since 17th June is appealing for help to find her, claiming she was forcibly taken from 9 Miles in Nakasi. Speaking to fijivillage News, Rizwan Ali claims his 38-year-old wife, Sophia Bi, was with their son and daughter when they stopped near Bhan's Car Sales in Nakasi to get a quotation.
item.content:encoded:
The husband of a woman who has been missing since 17th June is appealing for help to find her, claiming she was forcibly taken from 9 Miles in Nakasi.

Speaking to fijivillage News, Rizwan Ali claims his 38-year-old wife, Sophia Bi, was with their son and daughter when they stopped near Bhan's Car Sales in Nakasi to get a quotation.

Ali alleges that a man opened the vehicle door, forcibly pulled his wife from the car and threatened her and their son before taking her away in a taxi.

He claims his wife screamed for help, but no one was able to assist.

Ali says the incident was reported at the Nakasi Police Station on the same day.

He says the family has not heard from Bi since the incident and has also not received any updates from Police, leaving them deeply worried about her safety.

Ali is appealing to anyone with information about his wife's whereabouts to come forward and assist Police.

Questions have been sent to Police, and we are awaiting a response.

item.title: Former FijiFirst members register new political party, One Nation
item.description: Former FijiFirst members Faiyaz Koya, Adi Selai Adimaitoga, Sanjay Salend Kirpal and former FijiFirst Vice President Ravindran Kumaran have successfully registered as a political party after receiving approval from the Registar of Political Parties. Supervisor of Elections Ana Mataiciwa says One Nation has met all legal requirements for registration under the Political Parties (Registration, Condu
item.content:encoded:
Former FijiFirst members Faiyaz Koya, Adi Selai Adimaitoga, Sanjay Salend Kirpal and former FijiFirst Vice President Ravindran Kumaran have successfully registered as a political party after receiving approval from the Registar of Political Parties.

Supervisor of Elections Ana Mataiciwa says One Nation has met all legal requirements for registration under the Political Parties (Registration, Conduct, Funding and Disclosures) Act 2013.

Mataiciwa says the party resubmitted its application on 21st May this year after its initial application was not approved.

She says, as a registered political party, One Nation is required to comply with all statutory obligations under the Act, including submitting the required declarations and disclosures within the prescribed timeframes.

The registration of One Nation brings the total number of registered political parties in Fiji to 10.

item.title: Police investigate accident involving van, truck and car near Vesida
item.description: Police are investigating an accident near Vesida along Kings Highway earlier today. CCTV footage shows a black van crossing into the opposite lane and crashing into an oncoming vehicle before crashing into the concrete fence of a car wash.
item.content:encoded:
Police are investigating an accident near Vesida along Kings Highway earlier today.

CCTV footage shows a black van crossing into the opposite lane and crashing into an oncoming vehicle before crashing into the concrete fence of a car wash.

Following the initial collision, a 12-wheeler truck also collided with the same vehicle on the road.

Police confirm their traffic personnel are still gathering information.

item.title: Cunningham residents seek better security after years of theft
item.description: A Cunningham resident is calling for street lights and better security after her family experienced repeated break-ins and thefts over the past 10 years. Karolina Lomaibatiki, who has lived in Cunningham with her husband and children for the past decade, says theft has become a major concern for families in the area.
item.content:encoded:
A Cunningham resident is calling for street lights and better security after her family experienced repeated break-ins and thefts over the past 10 years.

Karolina Lomaibatiki, who has lived in Cunningham with her husband and children for the past decade, says theft has become a major concern for families in the area.

She says her home has been broken into three times and her family's vehicle has also been targeted.

She also claims cassava is regularly stolen from their plantation, with other families facing similar problems.

Lomaibatiki claims most of the thefts happen late at night, early in the morning or when parents leave home to pick up their children from school.

She says there have also been cases where cassava was stolen during the day while families were still at home.

Lomaibatiki says one suspect was caught, but she believes others are still responsible for many of the thefts.

She says another major concern is the lack of electricity and street lights along their road, making the area unsafe, particularly at night.

Lomaibatiki says residents have been requesting electricity and street lights for the past 10 years and have submitted several applications, but nothing has been done.

She believes installing street lights, including solar lights, would improve safety and help reduce thefts and grab-and-run incidents.

Lomaibatiki says during bad weather, families have to cross the road to charge their phones at homes with electricity. She claims some children have had their phones stolen while taking them to be charged.

She also claims groups of young men often gather along the dark roadside and residents are sometimes abused when they ask them to move on.

Lomaibatiki says she reported the cassava thefts twice at the Cunningham Police Station and reported a burglary at her home to Nabua Police Station in 2021.

She says Police visited after the burglary was reported, but there was no further follow-up.

Lomaibatiki says many residents now feel discouraged from reporting incidents because they believe little action is taken.

Questions have been sent to Police, and we are awaiting a response.

item.title: Fiji Community California raises funds to support vulnerable children and elderly
item.description: Vulnerable children and senior citizens in Fiji are set to receive greater support following a fundraising Kirtan event organised by the Fiji Community California, which brought together members of the diaspora community in a show of solidarity and giving back to their homeland. The event, which featured South African artist Preven Moodley, raised funds to assist some of the country’s most at-risk
item.content:encoded:
Vulnerable children and senior citizens in Fiji are set to receive greater support following a fundraising Kirtan event organised by the Fiji Community California, which brought together members of the diaspora community in a show of solidarity and giving back to their homeland.

The event, which featured South African artist Preven Moodley, raised funds to assist some of the country’s most at-risk groups, including children in need of care and elderly persons facing hardship.

Minister for Women, Children and Social Protection Sashi Kiran officiated at the event and thanked the organisers, supporters and overseas-based Fijians for their efforts in supporting social welfare initiatives in Fiji.

She says the gathering reflected the strong spirit of generosity within the Fiji diaspora and their commitment to supporting vulnerable members of society.

Kiran highlighted the increasing need for social protection services in Fiji, particularly for elderly citizens who face issues such as neglect, financial hardship, isolation and reduced family support.

She also expressed concern about the growing number of children requiring care and protection due to family breakdown, drug abuse, addiction and other social challenges.

The Minister says Government is continuing to strengthen its support systems through residential care facilities, community-based services, family support programmes and child protection initiatives.

She adds that work is ongoing to improve care facilities and ensure families receive adequate support so children and elderly persons can live in safe and stable environments.

Kiran says funds raised from the event will go towards improving care, wellbeing services and essential support for vulnerable groups.

She also encourages continued public participation in charitable initiatives aimed at strengthening social protection across the country.

item.description: Fiji Airways has marked World Environment Day by supporting a new native tree regeneration programme in collaboration with Animals

item.description: The Fiji Hotel and Tourism Association says the tourism industry did not propose, endorse or broadly agree to the Government's pro
